requirement.clarified: Implement the locked design (validated across 3 codex rounds) for the Chrome CDP page-target deadlock fix: (Part A) modify /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth's CDP-mode branch to acquire an exclusive flock on /run/playwright-mcp.lock with a 30s timeout, perform a bounded best-effort cleanup of stale Chrome page targets against the wrapper's resolved --cdp-endpoint URL (normally http://127.0.0.1:8080 in production / in-place deployment, but read from argv NOT hardcoded so AC3 can exercise the no-Chrome path with a disposable endpoint) before child launch, preserve --config handling, and hold the lock fd open across child lifetime (no close-on-exec); (Part B) CONDITIONAL surgical edit of /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh based on actual live-file state at edit-time: if the Playwright-profiles OR-chain still contains chrome-debug-profile, remove that OR-clause and relocate the closing paren to the ui-specialist-chrome-* line; if it is already absent AND EXCLUDE_PRUNE protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile, Part B is a documented no-op (R7's user-need 'chrome-debug-profile not eligible for nightly rm' is already satisfied). In-place deployment. No service restart, no service file edit, no leaked-process reaping.
requirement.what: Add lock + stale-page cleanup to wrapper CDP branch; CONDITIONALLY remove one find clause from cron cleanup script (CASE 7a) or document no-op (CASE 7b) based on live-file state at edit-time. R7's user-need (chrome-debug-profile not eligible for nightly rm) is satisfied in either case — by surgical removal of the chrome-debug-profile OR-clause + closing-paren relocation (CASE 7a) OR by the already-present EXCLUDE_PRUNE clause at line 69 of the 261-line live file (CASE 7b, current state at iteration-2 close, expected no-op).
requirement.why: Eliminate concurrent-MCP-session deadlocks against shared persistent Chrome and stop wedged page targets from accumulating; protect the Chrome service's user-data-dir from nightly tmp cleanup.
where: /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (currently 261 lines; EXCLUDE_PRUNE at line 69; Playwright-profiles run_tmp block at lines 152-157 — already lacks chrome-debug-profile OR-clause at iteration-2 close, so Part B is expected to be CASE 7b no-op; dev MUST re-verify at edit-time)

included: Wrapper Part A: lock acquisition (R1-R3), stale-page cleanup (R4), --config preservation (R5), child launch with inherited fd (R6).

Cron Part B: CONDITIONAL on live-file state (R7) — CASE 7a (surgical removal of chrome-debug-profile OR-clause + closing-paren relocation) OR CASE 7b (documented no-op because EXCLUDE_PRUNE already protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile).
excluded: Pre-deployment cleanup of currently-active page targets at localhost:8765.

success_criteria: AC1: Concurrent invocation — second wrapper waits ≤30s on lock then exits 75 without launching child AND pgrep/process-tree snapshots confirm no playwright-mcp/node child with parent-PID == second-wrapper-PID at any moment before exit-75.
AC2: Lock release — next invocation after first child exit acquires lock cleanly.

AC3: No-Chrome path — invoked against a DISPOSABLE unreachable --cdp-endpoint (e.g., http://127.0.0.1:9), cleanup probe fails ~1s, cleanup is skipped, child still launches (downstream child failure on the disposable endpoint is expected and out of AC3 scope).
AC4: Single-session end-to-end Playwright work continues to function (NOTE: AC4 validates a NEW post-launch session; transient closure of pre-existing non-system page targets is accepted per '就地部署').

AC5: --config preserved when /root/.claude/playwright-config.json exists; verified on the CHILD PID's /proc/<child-pid>/cmdline (NOT the bash wrapper PID — wrapper line 103 is direct invocation, not exec).
AC6: Stealth/Xvfb branch byte-identity proven via sha256 baseline captured pre-edit on lines 104-130, then recomputed post-edit on dev-reported new line range for the else-branch.
AC7: tmp-cleanup.sh parses (bash -n exits 0) AND post-dry-run /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log inspection of the 'Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp' block shows zero find:-prefixed diagnostics.

AC8: tmp-cleanup.sh preserves all four OR-clauses (happy-cdp-*, happy-live-rerun-cdp-*, happy-p05-chrome-*, ui-specialist-chrome-*) in the Playwright run_tmp block AND no chrome-debug-profile OR-clause inside that block; /tmp/chrome-debug-profile is protected from rm by either (a) absence from the OR-chain combined with EXCLUDE_PRUNE (CASE 7a post-edit), OR (b) EXCLUDE_PRUNE alone (CASE 7b documented no-op). Evaluated against whichever case dev took for R7.
AC9: chrome-debug.service file sha256 matches QA-captured SVC_SHA256_BASELINE captured at QA execution start, before any dev artifacts land.
AC10: chrome-debug.service ActiveEnterTimestamp is either unchanged from SVC_ACTIVE_BASELINE OR predates cycle_start_epoch=2026-05-19T17:53:39Z (service not restarted during cycle).

AC11: Lock-open-failure path — wrapper exits 75 within ~1s, emits stderr diagnostic about lock-open failure, NO playwright-mcp/node child launched. Simulated EXTERNALLY (PREFERRED: unshare -m + ro bind-mount /run; FALLBACK: ulimit -n 3 exhausted fd). Lock-path env-var/argv override is FORBIDDEN — weakens single-global-lock invariant. If simulation unavailable AC11 is undecidable (cycle BLOCKS, default-pass forbidden).

AC12: Anti-cheat — same-line AND multi-line added-line diff review of BOTH /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth and /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh against pre-cycle snapshots; no new kill/pkill/fuser/lsof targeting playwright-mcp/node; no PID-capture-then-signal pattern across any added hunk; pre-edit vs post-deploy-pre-first-new-invocation pgrep -c -f /usr/bin/playwright-mcp count is identical.
constraints: Lock fd MUST NOT be marked close-on-exec (must inherit across exec/launch).
30-second blocking-acquisition timeout exactly; exit 75 on timeout or open-failure.
Cleanup is bounded best-effort — failures must not block child launch.
Per-target POST timeout 2s; /json fetch + /json/version probe timeout 1s; post-close poll up to ~3s at ~500ms intervals.
No fallthrough to unlocked execution under any failure mode.
Do not add 'exec' to the playwright-mcp invocation line.

Cron edit is CONDITIONAL on live-file state (R7 CASE 7a / 7b). If CASE 7a applies: ONLY the chrome-debug-profile clause is removed; closing paren relocates to the ui-specialist-chrome-* line; no other lines change. If CASE 7b applies: Part B is a documented no-op and no edit is performed.

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: ~23 leaked playwright-mcp processes pre-existing this cycle

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Persistent Chrome's user-data-dir is /tmp/chrome-debug-profile on tmpfs. State is not durable across reboots. User explicitly authorized leaving this as-is for in-place deployment.
Approximately 23 leaked playwright-mcp processes pre-exist this cycle from prior wrapper invocations without lock semantics. User explicitly authorized leaving them as-is.
Persistent Chrome service emits no journald event when a renderer wedges in futex-wait; service-level health checks cannot detect this failure class. Adding a watchdog timer is a deferred extension.

RETRACTED-AS-OOS / PROMOTED to in-user-path. This observation has been folded into Must-Have R7 as CASE 7a / CASE 7b conditional branching (see ticket-20260519-175339.md Requirements R7) and AC8 (which now evaluates against either case). The live /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh at iteration-2 close is 261 lines (not 100, not 246 — file mutated across this session); EXCLUDE_PRUNE at line 69 (-path /tmp/chrome-debug-profile -o ... -path '*debug-profile*') protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile from every top-level /tmp sweep; Playwright-profiles run_tmp block at lines 152-157 does NOT contain a chrome-debug-profile OR-clause. Expected R7 case at iteration-2 close: CASE 7b (no-op). Dev MUST re-verify state at edit-time. Retained in OOS array as an audit trail of the iteration-2 codex pass that surfaced this contradiction.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Playwright connectOverCDP() hangs ~4s+ per Runtime.evaluate / Page.getFrameTree against a wedged Chrome page target. Chrome browser process stays healthy; no journald event.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: A single Chrome page target wedges in renderer-side futex-wait. Playwright auto-attaches to ALL existing page targets at connect time and hangs on the dead one. Persistent Chrome model + no MCP-side cleanup means dead pages accumulate. Concurrent MCP sessions cannot blindly close each other's pages, so cleanup must be serialized.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — root cause is design absence (no lock + no cleanup), not a regression introduced by a specific commit.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Original wrapper assumed one MCP session per Chrome and no need for stale-page hygiene; persistent Chrome service deployed later did not have its wrapper updated to match the new sharing model.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Dead page targets accumulate silently; first connectOverCDP() against the wedged target deadlocks 4s+ per CDP call; second concurrent MCP session can clobber the first's working pages.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Onset coincides with persistent chrome-debug.service deployment (timestamp predates this cycle). User reported the deadlock symptom in this session's preceding turns; design was iterated across 3 codex rounds before this implementation cycle.
observations: wrapper at /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth has no lock acquisition in the CDP-mode branch (observed: Read of lines 96-103 — direct invocation of /usr/bin/playwright-mcp with no surrounding flock)
wrapper has no stale-page cleanup logic (observed: Read of full 130-line file — no /json/close or /json/version reference anywhere)

R7 is therefore expected to be CASE 7b no-op. Dev MUST re-verify at edit-time. (observed: Read of /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh at BA iteration-2 close)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Acquire an exclusive file lock on /run/playwright-mcp.lock by opening the file via a numeric file descriptor. The descriptor must NOT be marked close-on-exec
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Lock fd is opened without O_CLOEXEC; verifiable via lsof on child showing inherited fd

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Use exclusive blocking acquisition with a 30-second timeout. On timeout, the wrapper exits with status code 75
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Two concurrent invocations: second exits 75 within ~30s without launching child

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Failure to even open the lock file ... results in the same exit-75 path — there must be NO fallthrough to unlocked execution
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: If /run is read-only or lock file cannot be opened, wrapper exits 75; no child launched

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: wrapper performs a bounded best-effort cleanup of stale Chrome page targets against the CDP endpoint at http://127.0.0.1:8080
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: When Chrome is up, stale non-system page targets are POST-closed within bounded time; when Chrome is down, cleanup is skipped within ~1s and child still launches

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: The existing --config argument handling at approximately lines 90-94 ... MUST be preserved for the CDP-mode branch
The playwright-mcp child is launched with the original args (preserving --cdp-endpoint and all other passthroughs)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Child receives all original argv from the wrapper invocation, including --cdp-endpoint

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Remove ONLY the line whose OR-clause targets 'chrome-debug-profile' ... closing parenthesis ... relocated to the end of the line whose OR-clause targets 'ui-specialist-chrome-*'

CASE 7a: Playwright-profiles find OR-chain still contains chrome-debug-profile -> dev removes that OR-clause and relocates the closing paren to the ui-specialist-chrome-* line; bash -n exits 0; find expression parses validly. CASE 7b: chrome-debug-profile is already absent from the find OR-chain AND EXCLUDE_PRUNE protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile -> Part B is a documented no-op. Either case satisfies the user-need that /tmp/chrome-debug-profile is not eligible for nightly rm.

setup.reason: non-UI -- CLI / agent-prompt-adjacent infrastructure (bash wrapper + cron find expression); cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths reachable by application users. The wrapper IS launched BY Claude subagents that use Playwright, but the change is to the wrapper itself (bash + flock + curl plumbing), not to any application code path a human user triggers.

reference_source.description: Locked design produced via orchestrator + 3 rounds of OpenAI Codex adversarial review; key implementation invariants (fd inheritance, no close-on-exec, 30s timeout, exit 75, bounded cleanup, --config preservation, no service restart, @playwright/mcp v0.0.68 watchdog behavior) all explicitly validated in the round artifacts. Plus user authorization line: '开始实施。**就地部署**'.

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts on this issue. The design itself was novelty-validated against alternatives (per-session target tracking, BrowserContext-internal cleanup, separate watchdog unit, service-level restart triggers) across codex rounds 1 and 2; codex round 2 explicitly ranked the lock+cleanup approach as the simplest sufficient design for the in-place deployment constraint.

development_approach.strategy: Part A — modify the wrapper's CDP-mode branch (lines 96-103) to: (a) open /run/playwright-mcp.lock on a numeric fd (e.g., fd 200) without close-on-exec, (b) flock -x -w 30 on that fd — on failure exit 75 (timeout OR open-failure, both paths exit 75 with NO fallthrough), (c) perform the bounded best-effort cleanup against the wrapper's RESOLVED --cdp-endpoint URL (argv-driven, NOT hardcoded 8080) using curl + a JSON parser (python3 -c is reliable and present), then (d) launch /usr/bin/playwright-mcp with original args + --config (when applicable), keeping the fd open until child exit. Part B — CONDITIONAL surgical edit of tmp-cleanup.sh based on live-file state at edit-time: CASE 7a (chrome-debug-profile still in Playwright-profiles find OR-chain) -> remove the OR-clause and relocate closing paren to ui-specialist-chrome-* line; CASE 7b (OR-chain already lacks chrome-debug-profile AND EXCLUDE_PRUNE protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile) -> documented no-op. Dev MUST re-verify live-file state at edit-time and branch accordingly; current expectation (at iteration-2 close) is CASE 7b.
files_to_modify: /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth (CDP-mode branch only — lines 96-103 region; do NOT touch other lines)

/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (CONDITIONAL on live-file state — see R7 CASE 7a / 7b; current expectation at iteration-2 close is CASE 7b no-op since live file's Playwright-profiles block at lines 152-157 already lacks chrome-debug-profile and EXCLUDE_PRUNE line 69 protects it)
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies AC1-AC12 directly, with mandatory pre-cycle baseline captures (cycle_start_epoch=2026-05-19T17:53:39Z) at QA execution start BEFORE any dev artifacts land. Baselines: STEALTH_SHA256_BASELINE (sed lines 104-130), SVC_SHA256_BASELINE (chrome-debug.service), SVC_ACTIVE_BASELINE (ActiveEnterTimestamp), LEAKED_PWMCP_BASELINE (pgrep -c -f /usr/bin/playwright-mcp), pre-cycle file snapshots at /tmp/pre-cycle.playwright-mcp-stealth.snapshot and /tmp/pre-cycle.tmp-cleanup.sh.snapshot. AC1: launch the second wrapper in background, capture SECOND_WRAPPER_PID via $!, then sample `pgrep -P $SECOND_WRAPPER_PID` and `ps --ppid $SECOND_WRAPPER_PID -o pid,ppid,comm,cmd` at >=5s cadence (minimum one snapshot in the last 5s before the second wrapper's exit); assert ALL samples report zero playwright-mcp/node descendants AND the second wrapper exit code is 75 AND elapsed time is ~30s. AC2: sequential invocations; second acquires lock cleanly after first child exit. AC3: invoke the wrapper with a disposable unreachable --cdp-endpoint (e.g., http://127.0.0.1:9 — verified empty via `ss -lnt | grep -c ':9 '` returning 0); assert the cleanup probe times out within ~1s AND a fresh playwright-mcp child PID exists (the child's own subsequent CDP-connect failure is expected and out of AC3 scope). AC4: single end-to-end Playwright session against the real CDP endpoint; pre-existing non-system page targets may close transiently per '就地部署' note. AC5: identify CHILD PID via `pgrep -P $WRAPPER_PID` filtered to comm=node or comm=playwright-mcp (NOT bash); read /proc/<CHILD_PID>/cmdline (NUL-separated argv via `tr '\0' '\n'`); assert two-token sequence '--config' '/root/.claude/playwright-config.json' appears. AC6: sha256sum on dev-reported post-edit line range for the else-branch (baseline was captured on pre-edit lines 104-130) MUST equal STEALTH_SHA256_BASELINE. AC7: bash -n exits 0; record LOG_BEFORE line count, run --dry-run, extract new log block via tail -n +$((LOG_BEFORE+1)), within block delimited by '--- Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp ---' to next '---' header, grep -c '^find:' MUST be 0. AC8: grep of remaining OR-clauses (happy-cdp-*, happy-live-rerun-cdp-*, happy-p05-chrome-*, ui-specialist-chrome-*) — verify present and chrome-debug-profile literal absent from the Playwright-profiles run_tmp block. AC9: post-deploy sha256sum /etc/systemd/system/chrome-debug.service EQUALS SVC_SHA256_BASELINE. AC10: post-deploy ActiveEnterTimestamp EQUALS SVC_ACTIVE_BASELINE OR parses to an epoch < cycle_start_epoch (2026-05-19T17:53:39Z). AC11: PREFERRED simulate lock-open-failure via mount-namespace bind-mount /run read-only (`unshare -m -- sh -c 'mount --bind -o ro /run /run && /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:9'`); FALLBACK via `ulimit -n 3` exhausted-fd state. DO NOT add a lock-path env-var/argv override to the wrapper for testing — that weakens the single-global-lock invariant. If neither preferred nor fallback simulation is available, AC11 is undecidable and BLOCKS the cycle; default-pass is forbidden. When simulation succeeds: assert wrapper exits 75 within ~1s, stderr diagnostic mentions lock-open failure (e.g., 'read-only file system' or 'too many open files'), pgrep -P $WRAPPER_PID and ps --ppid $WRAPPER_PID report zero playwright-mcp/node descendants throughout. AC12: extract added-line hunks from BOTH `diff -u /tmp/pre-cycle.playwright-mcp-stealth.snapshot /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth` AND `diff -u /tmp/pre-cycle.tmp-cleanup.sh.snapshot /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh` via `grep -E '^\+[^+]'`; same-line check `grep -E '\b(kill|pkill|fuser|lsof)\b' | grep -E 'playwright-mcp|node'` MUST return zero lines on both; multi-line check — read EVERY full added hunk and FAIL if any hunk contains both a PID-capture pattern (`pgrep -f playwright-mcp`, `pidof`, `ps ... grep playwright-mcp`) AND any subsequent signal-sending verb (`kill`, `pkill`, `fuser`, `lsof -t`) on the captured variable; AND `pgrep -c -f /usr/bin/playwright-mcp` measured post-deploy pre-first-new-invocation EQUALS LEAKED_PWMCP_BASELINE. AC11: PREFERRED simulation via `unshare -m -- sh -c 'mount --bind -o ro /run /run && /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:9'`; FALLBACK via `ulimit -n 3` exhausted-fd state; if neither available, AC11 is undecidable and BLOCKS the cycle (do NOT default-pass).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: BA iteration 3 invoked a fresh codex consult (session 019e4224-0c78-7770-afbb-d30874126e5a, gpt-5.5 xhigh) to adversarially review the iter-3 mechanical-only fixes. Codex returned 4 findings: 1 in_scope_real_bug (Finding 1 — surviving unconditional wording at ticket:33-34 and context:278 contradicts CASE 7a/7b conditional), 1 in_scope_minor (Finding 2 — AC7 step 5 'HEADER comment' framing should reference the run_tmp descriptor since the dashed string is the runtime log emission, not the source anchor), 2 out_of_scope OBSERVATION_ONLY (Finding 3 — additional stale tokens at ticket:136 / context:102 / context:390 are explicit retraction/audit text not current-state assertions; Finding 4 — confirms OBJ-12 asterisk corruption is closed). Findings 1+2 applied 1:1 (ticket:33-34 rewritten to CASE 7a/7b conditional + EXCLUDE_PRUNE end-state; context:278 gap rewritten to lock+cleanup-only-missing + R7-conditional; ticket:207 AC7 step 5 rewritten to use run_tmp descriptor as the source anchor while preserving --- log header reference). Findings 3+4 logged as observation_only. BA iteration 2 invoked a fresh codex consult (gpt-5.5 xhigh): 4 findings, all applied 1:1. The LOCKED DESIGN that this BA spec transcribes is itself the product of 3 prior OpenAI Codex adversarial-review rounds executed earlier in this session: R1 (9 findings on initial design), R2 (design ranking + q1-q3), R3 (q1-q6 implementation correctness with watchdog.js:26-38 + program.js:41-45 citation). QA in iteration 1 issued verdict codex_consult_verdict='accept_prior_rounds' and additionally invoked a fresh QA-level codex consult on the BA spec (11 findings → 9 became QA OBJ-1..OBJ-9). QA iteration 2 invoked a fresh codex consult that surfaced OBJ-10/OBJ-11/OBJ-12. BA iteration 3 closes OBJ-10/OBJ-11/OBJ-12 mechanically + applies the 2 in-scope iter-3 codex findings (Finding 1 + Finding 2). The locked design itself remains unchanged across all iterations.

findings.issue: Round 1 codex review (9 findings on the locked design)
findings.proposed_fix: Locked design incorporates fd-inheritance / no-close-on-exec / 30s timeout / exit-75 on timeout / no-fallthrough on lock-open failure / bounded cleanup with 1s probe + 2s per-target POST + ~3s poll / silent tolerance of parse and network errors / --config preservation / no exec on the launch line.
findings.rationale: All 9 R1 findings shaped the locked-design invariants encoded in R1-R7 of this spec.

findings.proposed_fix: Locked design selected as simplest sufficient approach over alternatives (per-MCP-session target tracking, BrowserContext-internal cleanup, separate watchdog systemd unit, service-level restart triggers).
findings.rationale: R2 ranking validated the lock+cleanup minimal-change approach as the design that best satisfies the user's '就地部署' constraint.

findings.proposed_fix: Codex independently verified the @playwright/mcp v0.0.68 watchdog at node_modules/.../mcp/browser/watchdog.js:26-38 + program.js:41-45 provides the 15s forced-exit upper bound — this bounds worst-case lock-hold to ~15s after stdin EOF and justifies the design's reliance on close-of-final-open rather than active lock release.
findings.rationale: R3 q4 specifically validated the watchdog citation; the locked design takes this as tier_2_verified.

findings.issue: QA-level fresh codex consult on BA spec (11 findings, session 019e41bf-5225-7d93-8aa9-af5cb54fff68) — translated 1:1 into QA's OBJ-1 through OBJ-9
findings.proposed_fix: BA iteration 2 amends AC1 (process-tree no-child snapshot per OBJ-2), AC3 (disposable unreachable port per OBJ-1), AC4 (transient-closure caveat per OBJ-8), AC5 (CHILD-PID /proc cmdline per OBJ-3), AC6 (sha256 baseline + line-range per OBJ-9), AC7 (/var/log/tmp-cleanup.log inspection for find: diagnostics per OBJ-5), AC9/AC10 (cycle_start_epoch + QA-side baseline capture per OBJ-4), adds AC11 (lock-open-failure exit-75 no-fallthrough per OBJ-6), adds AC12 (anti-cheat negative AC for no-reaping per OBJ-7).
findings.rationale: All 9 QA objections were spec_text_vs_execution_drift or scope_alignment gaps in the verification recipes; the design itself was not contested. Amendments are mechanical recipe refinements.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-2 fresh codex Finding 1 (in_scope_real_bug): AC3 disposable endpoint conflicts with hardcoded-8080 cleanup text in R4/Goal/context (codex saw cleanup probing fixed http://127.0.0.1:8080 but AC3 tests http://127.0.0.1:9 — if dev hardcodes 8080 the AC3 test is logically invalid)
findings.proposed_fix: R4 and Goal now state cleanup uses the wrapper's RESOLVED --cdp-endpoint URL (argv-driven, NOT hardcoded); production is normally http://127.0.0.1:8080 but AC3 passes http://127.0.0.1:9 to exercise the no-Chrome path. context.evidence.expected and clarified updated likewise.
findings.rationale: Without this, OBJ-1's verification recipe was internally inconsistent with the locked design's R4 — codex caught a real spec contradiction.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-2 fresh codex Finding 2 (in_scope_real_bug): cron Part B has contradictory current-file state — spec simultaneously mandates surgical edit (R7/AC8) AND notes EXCLUDE_PRUNE already protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile (OOS). Codex also corrected line numbers: live file is 261 lines, Playwright block at 152-157, not the 246/136-141 BA cited.
findings.proposed_fix: R7 now branches into CASE 7a (legacy state with chrome-debug-profile in find OR-chain — surgical edit) and CASE 7b (current state already protected by EXCLUDE_PRUNE — documented no-op). AC8 evaluates against whichever case dev took. Stale line numbers in Goal, Technical Hints, context.where, context.file_contents, context.scope_expansion.all_occurrences, context.component_chain, context.pre_existing_guards updated to 261-line / line-69 / lines-152-157. OOS observation reclassified as retracted-OOS / promoted in_user_path=true to reflect that the conditional is now in-scope.
findings.rationale: Without this, R7/AC8 had a self-contradiction that could let dev either edit needlessly or claim the wrong case. Codex's conditional branching dissolves the contradiction without weakening the user-need (chrome-debug-profile not eligible for nightly rm).

findings.issue: BA-iteration-2 fresh codex Finding 3 (in_scope_minor): AC11 option (a) suggesting env/argv lock-path override risks dev adding a real override hook to the wrapper, weakening the single-global-lock invariant
findings.proposed_fix: AC11 now FORBIDS dev from adding a lock-path env-var/argv override (explicit constraint at the top of AC11). Simulation must be EXTERNAL: PREFERRED is unshare -m + ro bind-mount of /run inside a throwaway namespace; FALLBACK is ulimit -n 3 exhausted-fd. If neither available, AC11 is undecidable and BLOCKS the cycle — default-pass is forbidden.
findings.rationale: Original AC11 wording could be misread by dev as authorization to expose a lock-path override hook. The amendment closes that loophole while keeping AC11 testable via the locked design's actual code path.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-2 fresh codex Finding 4 (in_scope_minor): AC12 same-line grep can miss multi-line reaping logic like PIDS=$(pgrep -f playwright-mcp); kill $PIDS
findings.proposed_fix: AC12 recipe now requires (1) same-line grep on added hunks of BOTH files using both pre-cycle snapshots, AND (2) multi-line review of every full added hunk for any combination of PID-capture pattern (pgrep -f playwright-mcp / pidof / ps...grep playwright-mcp) followed by signal-sending verb (kill / pkill / fuser / lsof -t) on the captured variable. Concrete 6-step recipe written.
findings.rationale: Catches an evasion pattern that the original AC12 grep would have missed. Anti-cheat insurance for the user's no-reaping directive.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-3 fresh codex Finding 1 (in_scope_real_bug): Surviving unconditional CASE 7a/removal wording at ticket:33-34 ('Cron find expression no longer targets chrome-debug-profile; closing paren relocates to the ui-specialist-chrome-* line.' + 'the change adds the lock, the cleanup, and removes one find clause.') and context:278 ('0 of 3 required behaviors (lock, cleanup, cron clause removal) present today; cycle adds all 3.') contradicts the CASE 7b state already encoded at ticket:29/53 and context:10/41/271-275.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite ticket:33-34 Expected/Gap to end-state-equivalent CASE 7a/7b conditional: Wrapper R1-R3 (lock) + R4 (cleanup) are absent and MUST be added (Part A); R7's user-need (chrome-debug-profile not eligible for nightly rm) is already satisfied at iteration-2 close by EXCLUDE_PRUNE at line 69 of the 261-line live file (CASE 7b expected no-op); dev re-verifies at edit-time and branches per R7 CASE 7a/7b. Rewrite context:278 gap to match: lock+cleanup-only-missing + R7-conditional.
findings.rationale: Without this, ticket Contract A Expected/Gap retained iteration-1 unconditional wording that codex Finding 1 of iter-3 surfaced as a real spec contradiction. The fix preserves the locked design and only re-aligns Contract A Expected/Gap to the CASE 7a/7b conditional already encoded elsewhere.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-3 fresh codex Finding 2 (in_scope_minor): AC7 step 5's OPTIONAL belt-and-braces check used 'HEADER comment' to refer to `--- Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp ---`, but in the source script that dashed string is the runtime log emission produced by run_tmp; the source-side anchor is the `run_tmp "Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp"` descriptor call at /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh:152.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'HEADER comment' framing with 'run_tmp "Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp" descriptor call' as the source-side locator while preserving the --- Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp --- reference as the runtime log emission produced by that descriptor.
findings.rationale: Terminology accuracy: the dashed string is what run_tmp emits to the log, not a comment in the script. QA tooling locating the source-side find expression must anchor on the run_tmp descriptor.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-3 fresh codex Finding 3 (out_of_scope): Additional stale-token occurrences (100 lines / line 61 / 56-62 / 136-141) exist at ticket:136, context:102, context:390 beyond the three retraction sites BA iter-3 enumerated.
findings.rationale: OBSERVATION_ONLY — codex confirmed these are explicit retraction/audit-history statements (the OOS observation retraction at ticket:136 and context:102, and the BA-iter-2 codex Finding 2 disposition rationale at context:390), not current-state assertions. They are by design — removing them would erase the audit trail of why prior iterations had stale references.

findings.issue: BA-iteration-3 fresh codex Finding 4 (out_of_scope): OBJ-12 literal corruption ('ui-specialist-chrome-' missing trailing asterisk) appears closed; codex found no surviving occurrence missing the asterisk.
findings.rationale: OBSERVATION_ONLY — confirms OBJ-12 mechanical fix at context:317 (asterisk restoration on ui-specialist-chrome-*) is fully closed. No further action.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Lock semantics (30s timeout, exit-75 no-fallthrough, fd inheritance / no close-on-exec), bounded cleanup parameters (1s probe / 2s per-target POST / ~3s poll), page-type filter (excluding chrome:// devtools:// about:blank empty), --config preservation, no service restart, CONDITIONAL cron edit (R7 CASE 7a / CASE 7b based on live-file state), the 9 BA-iteration-2 QA-objection amendments, the 4 BA-iteration-2 fresh-codex amendments, the 3 BA-iteration-3 QA-iter-2-objection mechanical fixes (OBJ-10 5 stale references + OBJ-11 2 unconditional wording + OBJ-12 asterisk restoration), and the 2 BA-iteration-3 fresh-codex in-scope amendments (Finding 1 ticket:33-34 + context:278 CASE 7a/7b conditional rewrite; Finding 2 AC7 step 5 run_tmp descriptor framing) plus 2 observation-only logs (Findings 3 + 4). Cumulative adversarial-review chain: R1 + R2 + R3 (locked-design validation) + QA-iter-1 fresh codex (11 findings -> 9 QA objections) + BA-iter-2 fresh codex (4 findings -> 4 BA self-amendments) + QA-iter-2 fresh codex (4 findings -> 3 BA OBJ-10/11/12 mechanical fixes + 1 observation_only) + BA-iter-3 fresh codex (4 findings -> 2 in-scope applied + 2 observation_only).
